title: gymnocarpium longulum
intro: Gymnocarpium longulum, also known as the long-beaked liverwort or fir club moss, is a species of liverwort that belongs to the family Gymnocarpiaceae. It is a small, perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Gymnocarpium longulum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
